title: Vilfa villarsii
intro: Vilfa villarsii, also known as V. villosa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Vilfa villarsii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to relieve coughs and congestion.
